sections.paragraph:
ἐπανήκω (ἀνήκω ‘to have come up to a point’) fut. ἐπανήξω come back again (since Eur., Iph. A. 1628, also Polyb. 6, 58, 3; SIG 730, 6; PAmh 50, 5; Sb 9123, 4 [both II b.c.]; PGron 18, 8; LXX; EpArist 8; Jos., Ant. 12, 179; 191) Hs 9, 11, 2.

fut. fut. = fut.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PGron PGron = Papyri Groninganae—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 359
